compiled_state.title: feat: add Resolve-and-fetch step to CI with atomic clone, sentinel, and SHA cache-validation
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
GREEN task (depends on e423-7c77): integrate resolve-dso-version.sh into the CI workflow template and/or a companion fetch script.

Changes:
1. Add a 'Resolve DSO version' step in plugins/dso/templates/ci-dso-staged.yml (or a new ci-dso-fetch.sh helper script) that:
   a. Calls resolve-dso-version.sh to get VERSION and SOURCE_TIER.
   b. Derives CLONE_DIR=${RUNNER_TEMP:-$(mktemp -d)}/dso/$VERSION/.
   c. Checks for sentinel file at CLONE_DIR/dso-sentinel.json (NOT existence of directory alone).
   d. Sentinel schema: {"version": "<ref>", "commit_sha": "<sha>"}.
   e. Before reusing cache: calls git ls-remote to verify recorded SHA matches resolved ref's actual HEAD SHA — mismatch triggers fresh clone.
   f. On cache miss or SHA mismatch: clones to a tmpdir, then renames into CLONE_DIR atomically (handles concurrent CI jobs).
   g. After clone: writes sentinel file containing version + commit SHA.
   h. Exports CLONE_DIR to subsequent steps.

Done criteria:
- Template or helper script contains Resolve-and-fetch logic.
- Sentinel check precedes directory existence check.
- SHA verification step present before cache reuse.
- Atomic clone pattern (clone-to-tmp, rename) used.
- Sentinel written after successful clone.
